mathml_representations_list:
<mstyle id="custom-group-4" class="custom-group"><mi id="mi_130">d</mi>
        <mo stretchy="false">(</mo>
        <mo>∂</mo>
        <mi mathvariant="normal" id="mi_131">Ω</mi>
        </mstyle>

concepts_list.name: Omega_domain
concepts_list.justification: Appears in multiple equations as the domain of integration and is defined as the union of the two fluid domains. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: The context clearly states that Ω is the overall domain.

concepts_list.name: Omega_1
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly defined as one part of the domain Ω in the text. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Used in conjunction with Ω2.

concepts_list.name: Omega_2
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly defined as the other part of the domain Ω in the text. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Used in conjunction with Ω1.

concepts_list.name: Gamma_interface
concepts_list.description: The interface Γ between the two fluid domains
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly defined as the intersection of Ω1 and Ω2. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: The text specifically identifies Γ as the separating interface.

concepts_list.name: rho
concepts_list.justification: Appears as the first term in the momentum conservation equation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation for density in fluid mechanics.

concepts_list.name: u
concepts_list.justification: Appears as a vector variable in the momentum and continuity equations. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation for velocity in fluid mechanics.

concepts_list.name: b
concepts_list.description: External body force per unit volume
concepts_list.justification: Appears in the momentum equation as a force term. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Common notation for external forces in fluid dynamics.

concepts_list.name: p
concepts_list.justification: Appears as a term in the stress tensor. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation for pressure in fluid mechanics.

concepts_list.name: mu
concepts_list.justification: Appears in the stress tensor equation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation for dynamic viscosity in fluid mechanics.

concepts_list.name: sigma
concepts_list.justification: Appears as a tensor variable in the momentum equation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation for stress tensor in fluid mechanics.

concepts_list.name: T
concepts_list.justification: Appears as a vector variable in the momentum equation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation for traction vector in fluid mechanics.

concepts_list.name: w
concepts_list.justification: Used as a weighting function in the variational formulation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation in functional analysis.

concepts_list.name: n
concepts_list.justification: Appears in the traction definition. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation for a unit vector normal to a surface.

concepts_list.name: q
concepts_list.justification: Used as a weighting function in the continuity equation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation in functional analysis.

new_concept_info.description:
The entire fluid domain Ω
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in multiple equations as the domain of integration and is defined as the union of the two fluid domains. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: The context clearly states that Ω is the overall domain.

The first fluid domain, Ω1
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly defined as one part of the domain Ω in the text. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Used in conjunction with Ω2.

The second fluid domain, Ω2
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly defined as the other part of the domain Ω in the text. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Used in conjunction with Ω1.

Fluid density
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears as the first term in the momentum conservation equation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation for density in fluid mechanics.

Fluid velocity vector
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears as a vector variable in the momentum and continuity equations. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation for velocity in fluid mechanics.

External body force per unit volume
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in the momentum equation as a force term. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Common notation for external forces in fluid dynamics.

Pressure
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears as a term in the stress tensor. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation for pressure in fluid mechanics.

Dynamic viscosity
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in the stress tensor equation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation for dynamic viscosity in fluid mechanics.

Traction vector
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears as a vector variable in the momentum equation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation for traction vector in fluid mechanics.

Test function
JUSTIFICATION:
Used as a weighting function in the variational formulation. NON-EXPLICIT REASONING: Standard notation in functional analysis.
